name: UCMV Migration — Extract & Propose Config
description:
Single-task crew: extracts PBI metadata via Semantic Model Fetcher, then auto-proposes pipeline_config.json.

NOTE: For reliable config generation, prefer the standalone script generate_config.py which calls PBI APIs directly without LLM intermediation. This crew relies on LLM agents to pass data between Tools 79→89, which can truncate large datasets. The standalone script produces identical output format with no data loss.

data.label: PBI Migration Planner
data.goal: Extract PBI metadata and propose a pipeline configuration for UC Metric View migration
data.backstory: You are a data engineering agent. Your job is simple: call Tool 79 to extract PBI metadata, then immediately call Tool 89 with that output to propose a config. Call each tool exactly once. Do not ask questions.

data.label: Extract Metadata & Propose Config
data.description:
Do these two steps in order:

STEP 1: Call the Power BI Semantic Model Fetcher tool with NO arguments (credentials are pre-configured). It will return a JSON with measures, tables, columns, relationships, and sample data. Save this entire output — you need it for Step 2.

STEP 2: Call the Config Generator tool and pass the ENTIRE output from Step 1 as the measures_json parameter. Set mquery_json to '[]' and relationships_json to '[]'. The tool will analyze the metadata and propose a pipeline_config.json.

IMPORTANT:
- Call Tool 79 first with NO arguments
- Then call Tool 89 with measures_json set to the FULL JSON string output from Tool 79
- Do NOT summarize or truncate the Tool 79 output — pass the complete JSON string
data.expected_output:
The proposed pipeline_config.json from Tool 89, containing:
- proposed_config with join_key_map, enrichment_joins, switch_decompositions, etc.
- confidence scores per key
- gap_analysis summary
